abstract: GILL--Arthur, 73 of Irvingtonon-Hudson, died September 7, 2004 after a lengthy illness. He was born in Brooklyn on February 4, 1931, the son of Estelle and Max Gill. He graduated from James Madison High School in 1948 and from City College of New York in 1952. He served in the U.S. Army during the Korean War. Mr. Gill was founder and president of Argil Electronics, a distributor of industrial electronic parts worldwide. His philanthropic work included presidency of the American Hostel Association in the 1960's and many organizations concerned with conservation of the environment. He was one of the founders and moving forces in the Arrowhead Group of the order of the Arrow, an honorary society of former boy scouts. The fact that thse men will be celebrating its 44th reunion at Ten Mile River this weekend is witness to Arthur's selfless dedication. He is survived by his wife, Carol Gill, and his two daughters Susan Gill and her husband Ronald Northrup of Sea Cliff; Barbara GillVayo and her husband William Vayo of White Plains; his two step daughters Deborah Jacobs and her husband Philip Jacobs of Fairfield, CT, and Marjorie Weston and her husband Brian Weston of Katonah and six grandchildren: Lucy Vayo, Marc and Alec Jacobs, Betsy, Caroline and Scott Weston. Funeral Thursday 1:00pm Temple Beth Shalom, 740 Broadway, Hastingson-Hudson, NY. Memorials in lieu of flowers can be made to the American Diabetes Association.

abstract: GILL--Madeleine Henriette. Of Water Mill, NY, and Staten Island, NY, on August 7, 2004. Beloved wife of the late Austin Gill. Loving mother of Helene A. Demisay. Dear mother-in-law of Nicholas Demisay. Cherished grandmother of Michael, Peter and Bernadette Demisay and greatgrandmother of Michelle, Danielle and Nicole Demisay. Madeleine Gill was a native of France. She was educated at the Sorbonne in Paris and held degrees in English Literature and Art History. She remarried in Scotland where she taught French at the University of Edinburgh. She was General DeGaulle's Representative in Scotland during WWII. She lived in Paris from 1945-1955 where her husband was the Director of the British Council. They lived in Oxford, England where her husband was a Professor of French at Magdalen College and where she taught French language. In the 70's, where they lived in Glasgow, Scotland, she was a special guide in the Glasglow Museum and the Burrell Collection, specializing in the art of the Middle Ages and French impressionist painting. She was an avid world traveler. She visited the United States in the summer for 30 years and came to stay permanently with her daughter in 1993. Reposing Harmon Home for Funerals, 571 Forest Ave, Staten Island, Sunday, 2-4 and 7-9 PM. Mass of Christian Burial, Sacred Heart of Jesus and Mary Church, Southampton, Long Island, Monday, 11 AM. Interment Sacred Heart of Jesus and Mary Cemetery, Southampton, Long Island.

abstract: SYMES--Margaret Gill. On May 24, 2004, in her 96th year. Formerly of Bay Ridge, Brooklyn; Tarpon Springs, FL, and Manchester Village, VT. Wife of the late William (1994). Beloved mother of Mary Symes-Leith, Nancy Symes Hersom and Kathleen Symes Junsch. Also survived by seven grandchildren and six great-grandchildren. Burial private in Arlington, VT, at a later date.

abstract: Organizers of Feast of San Gennaro in Lower Manhattan add five new board members, including former prosecutors, Nelson A Boxer and Austin V Campriello, hoping to allay concerns that organized crime once again taints festival; action follows resignation of Perry Criscitelli, festivals president, and his wife, Annette Sabatino, treasurer (M)

abstract: SHAPIRO--Dr. Joseph S. Age 85, of Columbus, OH, formerly of Paterson, NJ on February 8, 2004. Beloved and respected dermatologist. Survived by wife Elise, children Dr. Daniel (Sandra) Shapiro of Princeton Jct, NJ, Beth (Rabbi Harold) Berman of Columbus, OH, Dr. Lee Shapiro (Patrick Gill) of Albany, NY and seven grandchildren. For information, Schoem Funeral Chapel.
